transcript: of bread. All of the claims to the invention of the traditional hamburger are a bit shaky and happened around the same time, 1885 to 1900. Most of the claims come from transient state fare vendors that reportedly were looking for a way for fare goers to eat their meatballs on the go. Fletch Davis, the Menchez brothers, Charlie Negrin, and others have laid claim to the invention. However, the first established food cart to sell something named a hamburger sandwich was Louie's Lunch in New Haven, Connecticut in the year 1900. Probably the single most important moment in modern hamburger history was unquestionably the standardization of the hamburger bun by White Castle in 1920. From that point on, a patty between two buns became recognized around the world as a distinctly American dish called the hamburger.
